note_data.title: Chemical Bonding
keyPoints: Chemical bond is the force of attraction that holds atoms together in a molecule or compound
Ionic bond forms between metal and non-metal atoms through complete transfer of electrons
Covalent bond forms between non-metal atoms through sharing of electrons
Electronegativity difference determines the type of bond: >1.7 ionic, <1.7 covalent
Lewis dot structure shows valence electrons as dots around atomic symbols
Coordinate covalent bond forms when one atom provides both electrons for sharing
Metallic bonding occurs in metals through sea of delocalized electrons
Bond length is the distance between nuclei of two bonded atoms
Lattice energy is the energy required to completely separate one mole of ionic solid into gaseous ions

definitions.definition: Chemical bond is the force of attraction that holds atoms together in a molecule or compound.
Ionic bond is formed by the complete transfer of electrons from metal atom to non-metal atom resulting in the formation of ions.
Covalent bond is formed by the mutual sharing of electrons between two non-metal atoms.
Electronegativity is the tendency of an atom to attract shared electrons towards itself in a covalent bond.
Coordinate covalent bond is formed when both electrons of the shared pair are contributed by one atom only.

importantQuestions.question: Define ionic bond and give one example.
importantQuestions.answer: Ionic bond is formed by the complete transfer of electrons from metal atom to non-metal atom resulting in the formation of ions. Example: NaCl (sodium chloride) - Na loses one electron to Cl.

importantQuestions.question: Explain the formation of ionic bond in sodium chloride (NaCl) with Lewis dot structure.
importantQuestions.answer: Sodium (Na) has electronic configuration 2,8,1 with one valence electron. Chlorine (Cl) has configuration 2,8,7 with seven valence electrons. Na loses one electron to achieve stable octet, forming Na+ ion. Cl gains this electron to complete its octet, forming Cl- ion. The electrostatic attraction between Na+ and Cl- forms ionic bond. Lewis structure shows Na with one dot losing it to Cl with seven dots, resulting in Na+ and Cl- ions.

mcqs.question: Which type of bond is formed between Na and Cl?

commonMistakes: Students confuse ionic and covalent bonds - remember ionic involves complete transfer, covalent involves sharing
Drawing incorrect Lewis dot structures - only show valence electrons as dots
Forgetting to mention electronegativity difference criteria for bond types
Not showing electron transfer clearly in ionic bond formation diagrams
Mixing up coordinate covalent bond definition with regular covalent bond
examTips: This chapter usually has 2-3 MCQs and 1 short + 1 long question in BSEK papers
Lewis dot structure diagrams appear every year - practice drawing them perfectly
Memorize exact textbook definitions word-for-word for 'Define' questions
Learn electronegativity values of common elements like F=4.0, O=3.5, Cl=3.0, C=2.5
Practice drawing formation of NaCl, H2O, NH3, and CH4 - these are frequently asked
formulas: Lattice Energy ∝ (charge of cation × charge of anion) / distance between ions
note_data.keyDiagram: Lewis dot structure showing formation of ionic bond in NaCl: Na with one dot transferring electron to Cl with seven dots, resulting in Na+ and Cl- ions with complete octets. Also draw covalent bond formation in H2O showing oxygen sharing electrons with two hydrogen atoms.
pastPaperRefs: BSEK 2023 SSC-II Q4 (6 marks) — asked students to define ionic and covalent bonding with two examples each
BSEK 2022 SSC-II Q3 (4 marks) — required explanation of how atoms achieve stable electronic configuration through bonding
BSEK 2021 SSC-II Q5 (5 marks) — asked to differentiate between ionic and covalent compounds with properties
BSEK 2020 SSC-II Q2 (3 marks) — definition of chemical bond and why atoms form bonds

workedExample.question: Define chemical bonding and explain how sodium chloride (NaCl) is formed through ionic bonding. (6 marks)
steps: Define chemical bonding using STBB textbook definition
State electronic configuration of sodium (2,8,1) and chlorine (2,8,7)
Explain electron transfer from sodium to chlorine
Show formation of Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions
Explain electrostatic attraction between oppositely charged ions
State that this forms ionic compound NaCl
workedExample.answer: Chemical bonding is the force of attraction that holds atoms together in molecules or compounds. Sodium has electronic configuration (2,8,1) and chlorine has (2,8,7). To achieve stable octet configuration, sodium loses one electron to form Na⁺ ion (2,8) and chlorine gains one electron to form Cl⁻ ion (2,8,8). The electrostatic force of attraction between oppositely charged Na⁺ and Cl⁻ ions forms ionic bond, resulting in ionic compound sodium chloride (NaCl).
workedExample.markScheme: 1 mark for definition, 1 mark each for electronic configurations, 1 mark for electron transfer explanation, 1 mark for ion formation, 1 mark for electrostatic attraction explanation

examinerWarnings: Students often lose marks by writing general answers instead of textbook-exact definitions. Always use the STBB textbook phrasing for chemical bonding definition.
Many students confuse ionic and covalent bonding properties. Learn the exact tabular comparison from STBB textbook - don't mix up conductivity and solubility properties.
Avoid using complex terms not in the textbook. BSEK expects simple, clear explanations using only the terminology taught in Class 9 STBB book.
note_data.quickRecap: Chemical bonding is the attractive force holding atoms together. Ionic bonding occurs through electron transfer between metal and non-metal, forming oppositely charged ions. Covalent bonding involves electron sharing between non-metals. Atoms bond to achieve stable electronic configuration (usually octet rule).
